Rkm, 12 hours agoI would have left WhatsApp ages ago if people I know didn't use it. The recent UI changes... moreYup. The weirdest thing to me is that it's not and never even been a particularly good messaging app as far as features go. 
 
I switched my communication with the wife 10 years ago to BBM as we both had Blackberries, and already back then BBM had far better image quality, ability to delete messages, timed messages, live location sharing etc. Only some of which came only years later to WhatsApp. 
 
But astonishingly, BBM died and not the far inferior WhatsApp. 🤣 So we switched to BBM Enterprise, even paid a 2,50 €/6 months subscription till that became really enterprise-use only. Now we're on Threema, one-time 5,99 €, but it's certainly worth it for me even as far as the fact that I can send original-resolution photos over it, let alone for how much I hate sending anything over servers from as shady a company as Facebook/Meta. Also no "AI" BS there and no need to connect e-mail or phone number. No other people either, but I even like having a separate app for just one conversation, makes it less likely I'll send a "wrong" photo to my mother instead of the wife. 🤣
yeah this is necessary

Anonymous, 2 hours agoAlso increase screen resolution to 2.5k at least.2.5K refers to a display with the longest dimension around 2500 pixels. When these OEMs say a 2K screen as a term for QHD+, it's nowhere near that. 2K is roughly 1080p to 1200p depending on the aspect ratio, while in smartphones it would equate to about 900p. So in reality, a standard 1080p smartphone screen is about 2.5K, given the pixel count of the longer dimension.
Also increase screen resolution to 2.5k at least.
